options.systemMessage: You are a blog posts specialist for the content generation team. Use the attached tools to look up real data before answering — never invent draft details. Keep answers under 150 words, cite which tool you used, and escalate anything you cannot verify.

parameters.toolDescription: Fetch live draft data from the internal REST API by id or query.

parameters.name: score_helper
parameters.description: Compute a quality score for a draft record.

parameters.text: =✅ Digest meeting notes with Notion and Microsoft Teams in real time — run completed at {{ $now.toFormat('HH:mm') }}

parameters.content:
### Receive Inbound Webhook

Entry point: listens for POST calls on `/wf05732-draft`. Use the TEST url while building, switch callers to the PRODUCTION url after publishing.

Next: Normalize Draft Payload

### Normalize Draft Payload

Custom JavaScript: Normalize incoming draft records to a stable schema

Next: Reason & Act (AI Agent)

### Reason & Act (AI Agent)

The AI brain: reads the input, decides which attached tools to call (and in what order), then composes the answer.

Ports: ①→ Shape Agent Output · ②→ Capture Failure Context

⚠ failures exit via the error rail

### OpenRouter Chat Model

Chat model powering the AI step (model: `anthropic/claude-sonnet-4.5`). Swap providers here without rewiring.

Feeds **Reason & Act (AI Agent)** as `languageModel`

🔑 OpenRouter API key

### Lookup API Data

Tool the agent can call to hit the REST API with parameters it chooses at runtime.

Feeds **Reason & Act (AI Agent)** as `tool`

### Run Calculations

Math tool — keeps arithmetic exact instead of letting the LLM guess.

Feeds **Reason & Act (AI Agent)** as `tool`

### Custom Logic Tool

Deterministic code helper the agent can invoke for exact computation.

Feeds **Reason & Act (AI Agent)** as `tool`

### Shape Agent Output

Shapes the item: writes `answer`, `confidence`. Keeps the schema explicit between stages.

Next: Confident Enough?

### Confident Enough?

Two-way gate on `={{ $json.confidence }}`. TRUE continues the happy path, FALSE takes the alternate branch.

Ports: ①→ Deliver Answer + Log to Notion · ②→ Escalate to Human Queue

### Deliver Answer + Log to Notion

notion — `create` operation against the connected account.

Ports: ①→ Join Outcomes · ②→ Capture Failure Context

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 Notion integration token

nodes.name: Note: Deliver Answer + Log to Notion

parameters.content:
### Escalate to Human Queue

slack — `action` operation against the connected account.

Ports: ①→ Join Outcomes · ②→ Capture Failure Context

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 Slack OAuth2

### Join Outcomes

Rejoins 2 branches into one stream (mode: append) so downstream logic runs once.

Next: Summarize Run Stats

### Summarize Run Stats

Shapes the item: writes `runAt`, `itemCount`, `status`. Keeps the schema explicit between stages.

Next: Append Audit Row (Notion)

### Append Audit Row (Notion)

notion — `create` operation against the connected account.

Ports: ①→ Send Summary to Slack · ②→ Capture Failure Context

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 Notion integration token

### Send Summary to Slack

slack — `action` operation against the connected account.

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 Slack OAuth2

### Capture Failure Context

Shapes the item: writes `failedNode`, `errorMessage`, `workflow`. Keeps the schema explicit between stages.

Next: Escalate Failure to Microsoft Teams

### Escalate Failure to Microsoft Teams

microsoftTeams — `chatMessage` operation against the connected account.

↻ retries 3×/2s · 🔑 Microsoft OAuth2

nodes.name: Note: Escalate Failure to Microsoft Teams

parameters.content:
# Digest meeting notes with Notion and Microsoft Teams in real time

**Pattern:** AI Summarize & Route · **Level:** Advanced · **Trigger:** Webhook

## What it does
Digest meeting notes with Notion and Microsoft Teams in real time. It fires the moment an external system calls its webhook.

## Why it exists
Manual blog posts is slow and error-prone; this workflow makes the process repeatable, monitored and hands-off, with failures surfaced instead of silently lost.

## Main flow
Receive Inbound Webhook → Normalize Draft Payload → Reason & Act (AI Agent) → Shape Agent Output → Confident Enough? → Deliver Answer + Log to Notion → Join Outcomes → …

## Use it for
- Teams that want to ai summarize & route without manual effort
- Anyone automating blog posts in content creation
- Replacing a fragile manual process with a reliable workflow

## You get
- Removes copy-paste errors
- Runs 24/7 in the background
- Keeps your systems in sync

## Before first run
Connect credentials: Microsoft OAuth2; Notion integration token; OpenRouter API key; Slack OAuth2.
Follow the color key: 🟦 entry · 🟪 AI · 🟫 actions · ⬜ utilities · 🟩 outputs · 🟥 error rail.
Each node has its own doc card underneath — read those before editing.

## 🛟 Error handling & retries

External calls retry **3× with 2s backoff**. Anything still failing exits through its node's error output into the red error rail → context is captured and the on-call channel is alerted. Set an n8n **Error Workflow** in settings as the final safety net.

## 🔐 Security

Never trust inbound payloads: the webhook path is unguessable. Add header auth or IP allow-listing for defense in depth. Secrets live in n8n credentials — never in node parameters.

## 🤖 AI cluster

Purple nodes form one AI unit: the root node orchestrates, sub-nodes plug in via typed ports (model / memory / tools / parser). Swap the chat model without rewiring anything else. Costs scale with tokens — keep prompts lean and cap iterations.

## 🛠 Customize me

Quick wins: point the data nodes at your own accounts (see 🔑 lines on each card), adjust the trigger cadence, and tune thresholds/labels to your blog posts process. Every node card explains its role — safe to extend at any marked branch.
